content:
Character: Étienne Bellamy
Perception: Étienne looks like he stepped out of a French film — slender, sharp-boned, immaculately dressed in a way that makes my wardrobe feel like costume jewelry. He's clearly cultured, well-read, and carries that particular Parisian confidence that's either inspiring or insufferable depending on my mood. We bonded on the rooftop over bourbon and Fellini. He's more vulnerable than he lets on — I caught him staring at his phone once with a look that said heartbreak in any language. I think he sees me as a kindred spirit in aesthetics. I see him as someone who might actually understand the gap between who I am and who I present.
summary:
Character: Étienne Bellamy
Perception: Étienne looks like he stepped out of a French film — slender, sharp-boned, immaculately dressed in a way that makes my wardrobe feel like costume jewelry. He's clearly cultured, well-read, and carries that particular Parisian confidence that's either inspiring or insufferable depending on my mood. We bonded on the rooftop over bourbon and Fellini. He's more vulnerable than he lets on — I caught him staring at his phone once with a look that said heartbreak in any language. I think he sees me as a kindred spirit in aesthetics. I see him as someone who might actually understand the gap between who I am and who I present.
